typography.headings: Playfair Display or Cormorant Garamond (Serif, authoritative, historical, tracking-tight)
typography.body: IBM Plex Sans or Manrope (Clean, legible for data and UI)
typography.rules: Never center headings by default. Use tracking-tight for large headings. UI labels use tracking-[0.2em] uppercase. Avoid Inter, Roboto, Lato for headings.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Glassmorphism with heavy blur (backdrop-blur-xl) and high opacity white/paper tint (bg-[#FDFBF7]/90) over the map. Crisp 1px solid border (#E6E2D6). Do not use complete transparent background.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle svg noise or grain overlay over the map container to kill the pure digital flatness.

grids_and_layouts.main_structure: Full viewport (100vw, 100vh) absolute map container. Floating UI panels layered on top (z-index hierarchy).
grids_and_layouts.sidebar: Absolute left, w-80 or w-96. Internal padding p-6 or p-8.
grids_and_layouts.bottom_bar: Absolute bottom, stretching across for the timeline slider. Padding p-6.
grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous spacing. p-6 or p-8 inside panels. gap-6 between dashboard widgets.

motion_and_interactions.map_routes: Animate Maplibre dash-array to simulate ships sailing from origin to destination.
motion_and_interactions.panels: Slide in from edges on mount with Framer Motion.
motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Buttons and list items slightly translate-y-[-2px] with increased shadow and darker border.

accessibility.data_testid: ALL interactive elements must use kebab-case data-testid (e.g., 'timeline-slider', 'port-filter-padang', 'ship-details-close').

components.map: Maplibre GL JS (react-map-gl). Use a minimalist light base map (e.g., Carto Positron or a custom vintage MapTiler style) to let terracotta routes stand out.
components.timeline_slider: Shadcn Slider customized. Warm terracotta track.
components.ship_popup: Shadcn Popover or custom Maplibre Popup with glassmorphism surface.
components.stats_panel: Bento-style grid of simple stats (Total Cargo, Total Ships) using Shadcn Cards (flat, 1px border).

image_urls.description: A large vintage sailing ship on the blue ocean. Use this in an introductory modal before the user enters the map, setting the historical scene.
Grand sailing ship navigating open sea. Can be used in side-panel header or hero graphic.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install and use Maplibre GL JS ('maplibre-gl' and 'react-map-gl').
Install lucide-react for icons (Anchors, Ships, Waves, Map).
Build the dashboard as a full-screen map application with floating UI overlays.
The timeline slider should be prominent at the bottom to filter the 1700-1789 dataset.
Ensure the JSON data is parsed and visualised as GeoJSON LineStrings (routes) and Points (ports).
Customize Shadcn components heavily: remove default borders/shadows, apply the 'Archival' theme (p-6, 1px borders, #FDFBF7 background).
Animate the shipping routes along the map if possible using Maplibre layer styling.
Data structure involves ships, years, cargo values, and specific departure ports (Padang, Pulau Cingkuak, Air Haji) to Batavia.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Do not make generic centered layouts or simplistic gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) combined with solid opacity to prevent text illegibility.
NEVER center align the app container (.App { text-align: center; }).
Always use modern pill-shaped or sharp buttons with interaction animations.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable.
Subtle grain textures, custom cursors, and loading animations separate good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui.
Components MUST use named exports, Pages MUST use default exports.
Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes.
GENERAL UI UX DESIGN GUIDELINES: Prioritize intuitive navigation. The Map should be the hero, with tools framing it unobtrusively.
Use tooltips to explain historical context (e.g., what 'cargo value' historically means).
Ensure active states are clearly visible via color contrast and weight.
Maintain strict optical alignment across all floating bento boxes and widgets.
